title: Domino's Pizza dishes up 2Q earnings beat but misses on revenue
text: Domino's Pizza (NYSE:DPZ) has reported second-quarter earnings that beat expectations but revenue that missed the mark, sending its shares lower in Monday pre-market trading.  The Ann Arbor, Michigan-based company grew global retail sales by 5.8% in the three months to June 30, 2023, excluding the negative impact of foreign currency.

title: Domino's Pizza stock falls after profit tops views but revenue misses, as market basket pricing drops
text: Shares of Domino's Pizza Inc. DPZ, -0.62% dropped 1.9% in premarket trading Monday, after the pizza chain reported second-quarter profit that topped expectations but revenue that surprisingly declined, as market basket pricing to stores fell. Net income rose to $109.4 million, or $3.08 a share, from $102.5 million, or $2.82 a share, in the year-ago period.

(NYSE: DPZ), the largest pizza company in the world, announced results for the second quarter of 2023.

title: Uber's deal with Domino's is a strong endorsement, BofA analyst says as he turns more bullish
text: BofA Securities analyst Justin Post is the latest to weigh in with a more upbeat view of Uber Technologies Inc.'s stock UBER, +1.43%, lifting his price objective to $51 from $44. Uber recently announced a partnership with Domino's Pizza Inc. DPZ, +0.46%, and the pizza chain could emerge as one of Uber's highest-margin merchant partners, in Post's view, since Uber won't be actually delivering the orders.

title: 3 Dividend Stocks Poised To Outperform The Rest Of 2023
text: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq saw a significant rally in the first half of 2023, led by technology companies, with the top three performing sectors being Information Technology, Communication Services, and Consumer Discretionary. Given the top heavy rally in the first half, we go value hunting in today's piece and look at three quality dividend stocks primed to outperform in the second half. There is a lot of uncertainty in the back half of the year, which could have investors switch from risk-on assets to more defensive sectors.

title: Domino's Pizza Group appoints new chief executive officer
text: Domino's Pizza Group PLC (LSE:DOM) (DPG) has appointed Andrew Rennie as its new chief executive officer, with plans for the new boss to take over next month. Rennie joins the London-listed franchise after having previously worked in leadership roles at Domino's Pizza Enterprises, the Australian-listed franchise of the chain.

title: Domino's, Uber partner on pizza deliveries
text: Domino's Pizza customers can now order food through Uber Eats and Postmates apps beginning in four pilot cities, the pizza chain announced on Wednesday.

title: Domino's Pizza stock jumps on Uber partnership
text: Domino's Pizza Inc shares powered 12% higher in pre-market trading on Wednesday after the pizza chain announced that customers can order its products through Uber's Uber Eats and Postmates apps, in an effort to increase sales as its delivery business slows. Domino's said Uber Eats will be the exclusive third-party platform in the US until at least 2024, with the partnership kicking off in four pilot markets in the fall.

DPZ, +0.69% rallied 2.5% toward a five-month high in premarket trading Wednesday, after The Wall Street Journal reported that the pizza chain has signed a deal with Uber Technologies Inc. to have its menus listed on food-delivery apps. That marks a change from Domino's long-held stance against working with food-delivery companies, the WSJ report said.
